# Read the file content
path = "/mnt/data/file-TywBRAHufe49CDFELP5H89-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()

len(text)

import re, json, sys, textwrap

text = text  # from previous cell

def trim_edges(s):
    # Define characters to strip from edges (both sides), including whitespace
    strip_chars = ''.join([
        ' \t\r\n',
        '"\'«»()[]',
        '،؛:.,!؟?…',
        'ـ',
    ])
    return s.strip(strip_chars)

# Collect citations as dicts
citations = []

# 1) Aya markers between (aya_start) and (aya_end)
aya_spans = []
start_token = "(aya_start)"
end_token = "(aya_end)"

start_idx = 0
while True:
    s = text.find(start_token, start_idx)
    if s == -1:
        break
    e = text.find(end_token, s + len(start_token))
    if e == -1:
        # unmatched; break
        break
    # Matn between tokens
    matn_start = s + len(start_token)
    matn_end = e  # exclude end token
    matn = text[matn_start:matn_end]
    # Trim edges (whitespace, punctuation) - but likely none
    matn_trimmed = trim_edges(matn)
    # Adjust indices according to trimming
    # Find trimmed start relative to matn_start by rstrip in front and end
    # We can compute by comparing matn to matn_trimmed
    leading_len = len(matn) - len(matn.lstrip(' \t\r\n"\'«»()[]،؛:.,!؟?…ـ'))
    trailing_len = len(matn) - len(matn.rstrip(' \t\r\n"\'«»()[]،؛:.,!؟?…ـ'))
    # But this approach might mistakenly remove internal punctuation at middle. 
    # We'll recompute by searching matn_trimmed within matn and taking first match.
    inner_start = matn.find(matn_trimmed)
    if inner_start == -1:
        inner_start = 0
        matn_trimmed = matn  # fallback
    inner_end = inner_start + len(matn_trimmed)
    span_start = matn_start + inner_start
    span_end = matn_start + inner_end
    aya_spans.append((span_start, span_end, matn_trimmed))
    start_idx = e + len(end_token)

for span_start, span_end, span_text in aya_spans:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    })

# 2) Hadith markers between (hadith_start) and (hadith_end)
hadith_spans = []
hs, he = "(hadith_start)", "(hadith_end)"
start_idx = 0
while True:
    s = text.find(hs, start_idx)
    if s == -1:
        break
    e = text.find(he, s + len(hs))
    if e == -1:
        break
    matn_start = s + len(hs)
    matn_end = e
    matn = text[matn_start:matn_end]
    matn_trimmed = trim_edges(matn)
    inner_start = matn.find(matn_trimmed)
    if inner_start == -1:
        inner_start = 0
        matn_trimmed = matn
    inner_end = inner_start + len(matn_trimmed)
    span_start = matn_start + inner_start
    span_end = matn_start + inner_end
    hadith_spans.append((span_start, span_end, matn_trimmed))
    start_idx = e + len(he)

for span_start, span_end, span_text in hadith_spans:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth"
    })

# 3) Double parentheses ((...)) in the references section
# We'll find all occurrences of "((...))" and extract content inside without outer parentheses and whitespace/punct edges
double_paren_spans = []
pattern = r"\(\((.*?)\)\)"
for m in re.finditer(pattern, text, flags=re.DOTALL):
    content = m.group(1)
    # compute trimmed content and indices
    trimmed = trim_edges(content)
    # Exclude trailing question mark at end: our trim_edges already removes '?', '؟' at edges
    # Now locate trimmed within content to compute offsets
    inner_start = content.find(trimmed)
    if inner_start == -1:
        continue
    # compute overall start index in text
    matn_start = m.start(1) + inner_start
    matn_end = matn_start + len(trimmed)
    # Save this as hadith, but we need to ensure it is intended hadith.
    # Check context around it maybe; for this task we'll include all since they appear in [2] as hadith quotes.
    double_paren_spans.append((matn_start, matn_end, trimmed))

# Let's inspect what we found
double_paren_spans_texts = [text[s:e] for s,e,_ in double_paren_spans]
double_paren_spans_texts

for span_start, span_end, span_text in double_paren_spans:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth"
    })

# 4) The ayah in [4] after "حين قال: (" and before ")"
phrase = "حين قال: ("
idx = text.find(phrase)
ayah_additional = []
if idx != -1:
    open_paren_idx = text.find("(", idx)
    close_paren_idx = text.find(")", open_paren_idx + 1)
    if open_paren_idx != -1 and close_paren_idx != -1:
        content = text[open_paren_idx+1:close_paren_idx]
        trimmed = trim_edges(content)
        inner_start = content.find(trimmed)
        span_start = open_paren_idx + 1 + inner_start
        span_end = span_start + len(trimmed)
        ayah_additional.append((span_start, span_end, trimmed))
ayah_additional

for span_start, span_end, span_text in ayah_additional:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": span_start,
        "span_end": span_end,
        "span_text": span_text,
        "citation_type": "Aya"
    })

# 5) The hadith paraphrase after "فيقولون له:" (we need to ensure this pattern occurs in the text.)
pattern_phrase = "فيقولون له:"
idx = text.find(pattern_phrase)
hadith_quote_spans = []
if idx != -1:
    after_idx = idx + len(pattern_phrase)
    # Skip whitespace
    j = after_idx
    while j < len(text) and text[j].isspace():
        j += 1
    # The quote continues until "فيعتذر" that follows shortly
    end_marker = "فيعتذر"
    end_idx = text.find(end_marker, j)
    if end_idx != -1:
        # The quoted content is text[j:end_idx]
        content = text[j:end_idx]
        trimmed = trim_edges(content)
        # Determine trimmed indices relative to text
        inner_start = content.find(trimmed)
        span_start = j + inner_start
        span_end = span_start + len(trimmed)
        hadith_quote_spans.append((span_start, span_end, trimmed))
hadith_quote_spans

# Sort citations by start index for readability
citations_sorted = sorted(citations, key=lambda x: x["span_start"])

def show_context(span_start, span_end, width=40):
    start = max(0, span_start - width)
    end = min(len(text), span_end + width)
    ctx = text[start:end]
    # highlight span by replacing with brackets? We'll just show substring with context markers
    return ctx, text[span_start:span_end]

for c in citations_sorted:
    ctx, span = show_context(c["span_start"], c["span_end"])
    print(c["citation_type"], c["span_start"], c["span_end"], repr(span))
    print(ctx)
    print("-" * 80)

content.text: {"citations":[{"span_start":239,"span_end":385,"span_text":"وَلَمَّا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ وَاسْتَوَىٰ أَتْلُجَ الْبَيْتَ حِينَ بَلَغَ أَنِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبَّكُمْ ۖ إِنَّنِي خَشِيتُ أَن تَقْتُلُونِ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":594,"span_end":667,"span_text":"أَنْتَ آدَمُ الَّذِي أَغْوَيْتَ النَّاسَ وَأَخْرَجْتَهُمْ مِنَ الْجَنَّةِ","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":988,"span_end":1026,"span_text":"وَلَا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ","citation_type":"Aya"},{"span_start":1657,"span_end":1699,"span_text":"أنت آدم الذي أغويت الناس وأخرجتهم من الجنة","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":1725,"span_end":1815,"span_text":"أنت آدم أبو البشر، خلقك الله بيده، وأسجد لك ملائكته، ثم تخرج الناس من الجنة تأكل من الشجرة","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":2025,"span_end":2129,"span_text":"قال له موسى أنت آدم الذي أغويت الناس وأخرجتهم من الجنة؟ فقال له آدم: أنت موسى الذي أعطاه الله علم كل شيء","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":2150,"span_end":2180,"span_text":"أنت موسى الذي أعطاه علم كل شيء","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":2204,"span_end":2270,"span_text":"أن الخضر قال له: ما علمي وما علمك إلى كما نقص هذا العصفور من البحر","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":3228,"span_end":3277,"span_text":"أنت أول رسول بعثه الله إلى الأرض اشفع لنا عند ربك","citation_type":"Hadeeth"},{"span_start":3331,"span_end":3428,"span_text":"فَقَالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ ابْنِي مِنْ أَهْلِي وَإِنَّ وَعْدَكَ الْحَقُّ وَأَنْتَ أَحْكَمُ الْحَاكِمِينَ","citation_type":"Aya"}]}
